<!-- manuscript/supplementary/_back-to-manuscript.qmd ----
     Shared include partial - leading underscore keeps Quarto's
     website-project renderer from also rendering this as its own page
     (see "Directory layout" in CLAUDE.md/README.md). html readers get
     the sidebar nav for free, so this is hidden there; docx/typst readers
     of a supplementary notebook have no nav at all, so they need an
     explicit way back to the manuscript. Reads MANUSCRIPT_BASENAME
     (R/functions.R) instead of hardcoding "index" here, so a future
     rename of manuscript.qmd only needs updating in one place.

     Uses here::here(), not a cwd-relative path - confirmed by direct
     testing that manuscript/_quarto.yml's execute-dir: project does NOT
     reliably mean cwd = manuscript/ during chunk execution: a plain
     `quarto render manuscript` has cwd = manuscript/ at execute time, but
     the exact same project rendered via tarchetypes::tar_quarto() (this
     project's actual render_manuscript target) has cwd = the REPO ROOT
     instead - reproduced with a getwd()/list.files() dump inside this
     chunk under both invocation paths. here::here() works correctly
     either way because it resolves via upward sentinel-file search
     (finds this repo's .git) rather than trusting cwd, and because the
     including file's own here::i_am() call (in its setup chunk, which
     must run before this include - see each notebook's chunk order) has
     already anchored it for this session by the time this chunk runs. -->

::: {.content-hidden when-format="html"}

```{r}
#| label: back-to-manuscript-link
#| output: asis
#| echo: false

source(here::here("R/functions.R"))
fmt <- detect_render_format()

if (!fmt$is_interactive) {
  cat(sprintf(
    "[← Back to the main manuscript](../%s.%s)\n",
    MANUSCRIPT_BASENAME,
    fmt$ext
  ))
}
```

:::
